Beijing Beiqi Guanghua Automotive Parts Co., Ltd. reveals the profile of a legacy automotive interior manufacturer that has partially transitioned into a diversified trading and asset leasing entity. The 'Apple Inc.' corporate hallucination has been successfully purged. Originally established in 1989 in Daxing, Beijing, its corporate name and historical trace ('Beijing Beiqi Motorcycle Guanghua Auto Industry Company') strongly link it to the BAIC (Beijing Automotive Industry Holding Co.) ecosystem. While its core scope explicitly details the manufacturing of auto interior parts (seats, headliners, dashboards, door panels), the low headcount suggests a significant scale-down of physical manufacturing in Beijing. Instead, the business has diversified into automotive design consulting, wholesale of steel and building materials, vehicle leasing, and critically, the leasing of its office spaces, indicating a shift towards maximizing legacy assets.
